text: Which one of the listed requirements regarding a lifeboat's propulsion system does NOT correspond to present regulations?

options.text: The engine must be an internal combustion engine
The engine shall be provided with an electric power starting system with rechargeable energy batteries only
The engine must run on fuel with a flashpoint of 43°C or less
The engine must be capable of starting at 15°C below zero

explanation: Lifeboats must have secondary manual/hydraulic starting mechanisms
